fact: June 30 is exactly halfway through the year! If the year were a pizza, you'd have eaten exactly half by now.
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when fireflies are having their biggest glow parties of the summer, blinking like tiny disco balls in your backyard.
The sun sets at its latest time of the year around June 30 in many places - even though the longest day already passed, sunset keeps getting later for about a week!
Baby raccoons are just starting to leave their dens for the first time around this date, stumbling around like furry toddlers learning to walk.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, June 30 marks the dead of winter - kids are bundled up in snow gear while you're thinking about swimming!
Wild strawberries reach peak ripeness right around now in many forests, turning the ground into nature's candy store.
The zodiac switches from Gemini to Cancer on this day, which means if you're born on June 30, you're right on the cosmic border between two star signs.
Cicadas are screaming their loudest around this time of year - some make sounds as loud as a motorcycle revving up!
In northern places like Alaska, the sun barely sets at all on June 30, creating an eerie twilight that lasts all night long.
Rose petals are at their most fragrant around this date because the heat makes their oils extra strong - it's like nature's perfume factory is working overtime.
June 30 falls exactly 10 days after the summer solstice, which means Earth is still tilted at almost its maximum angle toward the sun.
Bats are having their busiest hunting nights around now because there are more bugs flying around than any other time of year - it's like an all-you-can-eat buffet in the sky!
